subject: Product Management
title: The Rise of Software PMs
description: Learn The Rise of Software PMs in one minute.

hook.text: Microsoft hired its first product managers in the late 1980s — and gave them more power than anyone expected.

buildup.text: At Microsoft, PMs owned the product vision and wrote the spec. Engineers built what the PM defined.

discovery.text: This was radical: at most companies, engineering decided what to build. Microsoft gave that power to PMs.

twist.text: Google later flipped the model: engineers drove decisions, and PMs influenced through data and persuasion.

climax.text: Two competing models — PM-led and engineering-led — still define how tech companies build products today.

punchline.text: Who decides what to build? That question defines a company.

quiz.question: How did Microsoft's PM model differ from Google's?
options: Microsoft PMs owned the spec; Google PMs influenced through data and persuasion
